codeReview.summary: The rerun removed source-text scanning and added runtime-invoked refreshAll tests, but the new assertions do not prove the required commit-coupled advancement semantics end-to-end. The success-path test is non-discriminating, and the failure-path test can pass without demonstrating that refreshAll skipped runScheduledBackfillIfNeeded due to persistence failure.

issues.description: `test_refreshAll_withSuccessfulPersistence_allowsCursorAdvance` only asserts cursor existence (`XCTAssertNotNil(finalCursor)`), but the cursor is already created before `refreshAll()`. This does not validate that `runScheduledBackfillIfNeeded()` actually advanced the cursor on successful persistence, so the required success-path behavior is unproven.

`test_refreshAll_withPersistenceFailure_preventsCursorAdvance` makes the DB read-only and then infers guard behavior from unchanged cursor state, but it does not assert that the persistence write in `refreshAll()` failed in the guarded section. Because `refreshAll()` inserts `allUsages` (`UsageAggregator.swift:222`) and parsers may yield no rows, the insert path can be a no-op while cursor non-advancement is instead caused by read-only write failure during cursor update, not by `if persistenceError == nil` gating (`UsageAggregator.swift:242`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Procedure-evidence enforcement is still weak for token-accounting-worker handoffs marked `followedProcedure=true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: This handoff includes evidence for reading `mission.md` and `AGENTS.md` only (`head -n 3 mission.md && grep -n 'fulfills' AGENTS.md | head -n 5`), while `token-accounting-worker` step 1 also requires reading `.factory/library/architecture.md` and `.factory/library/user-testing.md`.

summary: Fail: source-text scanning was removed, but the replacement tests still do not conclusively verify commit-coupled cursor advancement behavior across refreshAll success and persistence-failure paths.
